notes: Tests whether skill enforces SUCCESs checklist (Simple/Unexpected/Concrete/Credible/Emotional/Stories) vs baseline generic 'make it catchy' advice.

universal_assertions.text: Explicitly names the SUCCESs principles (at least 4 of the 6: Simple, Unexpected, Concrete, Credible, Emotional, Stories)
References or applies the Curse of Knowledge concept
Pushes concrete sensory language over abstract jargon

evals.name: diagnose-weak-message
evals.prompt: Here's the current tagline I'm testing: 'Rubinstein Productions empowers mission-driven organizations to amplify their authentic voice through innovative storytelling solutions.' Tell me what's wrong with it and why it won't stick.

fixture_assertions.text: Scores the tagline against the SUCCESs principles explicitly (not just generic critique)
Flags abstract/jargon words like 'empowers', 'authentic', 'innovative solutions' as failures of Concrete/Simple

evals.name: craft-sticky-tagline
evals.prompt: Give me a sticky tagline for Say Why — the practice of helping leaders stop sounding like their sector and start sounding like themselves.

fixture_assertions.text: The proposed tagline is concrete and sensory, not abstract
Includes an unexpected twist, reversal, or counterintuitive element (the U in SUCCESs)
